_notes: CLIENT RULES (locked): (1) NO FEET - figures' legs end in plain rounded stumps, no feet/shoes, no ground line, no shadow; enforce in every plate prompt. (2) ALL 16:9 - every clip renders 16:9; do not use 9:16/1:1. (3) FIGURE ONLY, NO BACKGROUND - generate on a solid green screen (chromaColor #00B140) then key it out with scripts/chroma_key.ps1 to a transparent WebM (defaultOutput=transparent). Also keep the green-screen MP4 as a universal fallback. Avoid green accents in recipes (they'd be keyed out) - dropped the green from accentColors. MODEL TIERS: model=seedance1_5 (Seedance 1.5 Pro) for SIMPLE single-figure whole-body actions (~5cr; durations 4/8/12); complexModel=kling2_6 for COMPLEX (2+ figures, effects, small fast objects/physics; ~5cr, 1080p; durations 5/10); premiumModel=veo3_1 (Veo 3.1 full, ~22cr, quality high) ONLY as an approval-gated escalation for the absolute cleanest; fallbackModel=seedance_2_0. RETIRED/AVOID: seedance_1_5 (gone from Higgsfield), kling3_0_turbo (weak motion, drops small objects). BALL-KICK LESSON: a small fast object being struck/launched (a kicked ball) is the hardest beat and seedance CANNOT do it (the ball vanishes/explodes); kling2_6 and veo3_1 CAN, so route any strike/launch/projectile physics to complexModel. COST GATE: classify each build SIMPLE vs COMPLEX; for COMPLEX (or get_cost >= costGate.thresholdCredits, or count:2, or 8s+, or premiumModel) preflight get_cost, show credits + dollars, warn that physics may need rerolls, and get explicit approval before spending. GREEN MUST BE FLAT: prompts say a single uniform flat chroma green, no horizon/field/two-tone/gradient (the green-dominance key removes a two-tone green anyway, but a flat source also helps editor-side keying). Plate prep recolors to navy #1A2238.
